claimText: To transition to a universal bank, a Small Finance Bank must have a minimum net worth of ₹1,000 crore and a scheduled status for at least 5 years.
evidenceExcerpt: Transition requires 5 years of scheduled status, ₹1,000 crore minimum net worth, and stock exchange listing.
examUse: Statement-based question on SFB transition criteria.

claimText: The asset quality requirement for an SFB to transition to a universal bank mandates Gross NPA of 3% or less and Net NPA of 1% or less over the last two financial years.
evidenceExcerpt: Asset quality for transition requires Gross NPA ≤ 3% and Net NPA ≤ 1% over the last two FYs.
examUse: Statement-based question on asset quality requirements for universal bank transition.

claimText: Small Finance Banks are registered under the Companies Act, 2013 and are licensed under Section 22 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949.
evidenceExcerpt: SFBs are registered under the Companies Act, 2013 and licensed under Section 22 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949.
examUse: Statement-based question on the legal and regulatory framework of SFBs.
distractorHooks: Licensed under the Payment and Settlement Systems Act, 2007

claimText: The minimum paid-up voting equity capital required for Small Finance Banks is ₹200 crores.
evidenceExcerpt: Minimum paid-up voting equity capital for SFBs is ₹200 crores.
examUse: Statement-based question on capital requirements for setting up SFBs.
